outcome: Mobility: Timed Up and Go Test post intervention
source_quote: The paper does not report a Timed Up and Go Test. It states: 'the dynamic analysis of balance and gait is evaluated by means of the Tinetti scale in our study, whereas other authors focused on the Timed Up & Go (TUG) and the 10 Meters Walk Test (10 MWT), therefore hindering any potential comparison.'
Table 1: Balance (Berg) - EG Post: 46.00 +/- 13.08, CG Post: 28.91 +/- 20.40, p=0.007. N=12 per group.

outcome: Exacerbations requiring a course of oral corticosteroids: subgrouped by time frame of follow-up
source_quote: Table 2: Prednisone bursts OR at 24-months, CHW vs AE-C intervention difference: OR 0.35 (95% CI 0.15, 0.81), P<0.05. At 12-months: OR 0.67 (95% CI 0.36, 1.26). CHW baseline proportion 0.56, AE-C 0.69; CHW 12-mo 0.26, AE-C 0.22; CHW 24-mo 0.32, AE-C not separately listed.

outcome: Exacerbations leading to emergency department visits
source_quote: Table 2: ED visit in the past 6 mo (>=1 time) at 12-month follow-up: INT group 27/95 (28.4%), CON group 40/86 (46.5%). RR 1.60 (95% CI 1.08-2.37).

outcome: Exacerbations requiring a course of oral corticosteroids
source_quote: Table 2: No. of oral corticosteroid prescriptions (quick relief) over 12-mo follow-up: INT 1.16 (95% CI 0.84-1.48), CON 1.20 (95% CI 0.88-1.52), overall mean (SD) = 1.20 (1.50). Wilcoxon z=0.48, P=.63.
Table 2: Hospitalized in the past 6 mo (>=1 time) at 12-month follow-up: INT group 4/95 (4.2%), CON group 11/86 (12.8%). RR 3.03 (95% CI 1.00-9.18).
